summary:
The available literature does not evaluate how changes in nerve length influence radiotherapy results for patients undergoing radiosurgery for trigeminal neuralgia; instead, research focuses on dosing parameters and symptom response patterns. \boxed{No} 

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only clinically significant information from the provided context.)

The reviewed scientific literature contains no data evaluating whether increases in nerve length within the radiation field correlate with improved radiological or symptomatic outcomes in trigeminal neuralgia treatments. Clinical focus remains primarily on factors such as prescribed doses, time-to-pain-relief onset, and adverse effects—none addressing anatomy like nerve dimensions directly.\boxed{No}

1) There is zero evidence linking nerve length variation to outcome improvement — this is explicitly noted across multiple sources being evaluated.  
2) Research exclusively addresses other variables (dose level, pain timeline), never anatomical features like nerve size/length itself.
